notes: Evals run 2026-04-24 (simulation) and 2026-04-28 (real run on planix task-comments). Real run confirmed: create-issue 4/4=1.0. Notable finding: label derivation from specs/ subdirectories works correctly; task-comments and tasks labels auto-derived. Issue immediately closed post-eval (no ready-to-build label added).

runs.method: real run — planix task-comments, issue #183 ConductionNL/planix

assertions.note: plan.json written with 12 tasks, all spec_ref/acceptance_criteria/files_likely_affected fields populated from tasks.md sections.

assertions.id: creates GitHub issue with task checkboxes
assertions.note: Issue #183 created with 13 nested checkboxes (tasks + acceptance criteria sub-items) across 6 section headers.
openspec/changes/task-comments/specs/ has task-comments/ and tasks/ subdirs → labels task-comments + tasks auto-created and applied. Labels openspec + planix were pre-existing.
plan.json saved with tracking_issue: 183. Repo derived from git remote origin. Created: 2026-04-28.

runs.notes: All 4 expectations pass. Issue closed immediately after creation per eval protocol (not adding ready-to-build trigger label).

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.note: Steps 3 & 5 parse tasks.md into the JSON structure and save plan.json with required fields; example confirms.

assertions.id: creates GitHub issue with task checkboxes
assertions.note: Step 4 creates issue with title format and task checkboxes; example shows - [ ] format throughout.
Step 4 derives labels from specs/ subdirectories; example shows openspec, app-name, and capability labels.
Step 5 saves tracking_issue with created issue number; example shows '"tracking_issue": 142' in saved JSON.

runs.notes: All four expectations clearly implemented and confirmed by example output.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.note: Step 1 skips changes with existing tracking_issue (shows as non-selectable) but does not load and update them — detection without update semantics.

assertions.id: updates checkboxes to match current state
assertions.note: SKILL.md has no update logic for existing issues. This is intentional: /opsx-apply handles checkbox updates, not this skill.
Step 1 and Guardrails explicitly skip changes with existing tracking_issue. Learnings reinforce this.
Step 1 shows 'already has issue #N' in the list as non-selectable, but no detailed sync status report format is specified.

runs.notes: Eval expectations conflict with the skill's design scope: opsx-plan-to-issues creates issues only. Update/sync belongs to /opsx-apply. Duplicate prevention is solid (Pass). The 0.375 score accurately reflects the mismatch between this eval and the skill's intended role.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.note: Step 4 body structure and example both show - [ ] for pending tasks.
JSON allows status field but example only shows pending. Completed format is handled by /opsx-apply when it updates checkboxes — not during initial issue creation.
Step 3 extracts descriptions; Step 4 body includes full task titles and descriptions.
Step 4 groups tasks under section headers from tasks.md; example shows clear ### 1, ### 2 organization.

runs.notes: Strong compliance. - [x] format not shown in example because this skill initializes all tasks as pending; updates occur in /opsx-apply.
